name: Post-Employment Non-Compete
text: The Employee acknowledges that, given the highly confidential and sensitive nature of the Employer's business and the Employee's access to proprietary information, the following limited restrictions are reasonable and necessary to protect the Employer's legitimate business interests. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Employee and the Employer agree that post-employment non-compete restrictions are limited under Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and accordingly: (a) during the term of employment, the Employee shall not engage in any activity competitive with the Employer's business; and (b) for a period of twelve (12) months following termination of employment, the Employee shall not solicit the Employer's clients or use the Employer's Confidential Information for the benefit of a competitor. The Parties acknowledge that blanket post-employment non-compete clauses are void under Indian law, and this clause is limited to the protection of Confidential Information and client relationships.
invalid_if: Post-employment non-compete extends beyond protection of Confidential Information – likely void under ICA S.27
Geographic restriction unreasonably broad without nexus to employer's actual market
title: Post-Employment Non-Compete
